title_en: Platform Illiberalism on X: Governance, Visibility, and Political Signaling
title_zh: Platform Illiberalism on X: Governance, Visibility, and Political Signaling
abstract_en: Using a case study of policy change, ranking signals, and public reaction on X, the paper argues that governance redesign can normalize asymmetrical visibility. The authors describe how moderation, recommendation, and symbolic leadership signals combine to reorganize who appears legitimate in platform publics.
abstract_zh: Using a case study of policy change, ranking signals, and public reaction on X, the paper argues that governance redesign can normalize asymmetrical visibility. The authors describe how moderation, recommendation, and symbolic leadership signals combine to reorganize who appears legitimate in platform publics.
plain_summary_zh: This paper examines how changes in platform rules also change what people see and who gets visibility. Using X as a case, the authors argue that platforms are not neutral spaces: governance choices themselves send political signals.
plain_summary_en: This paper examines how changes in platform rules also change what people see and who gets visibility. Using X as a case, the authors argue that platforms are not neutral spaces: governance choices themselves send political signals.
research_direction_zh: It sits in the research area linking platform governance to democratic life, especially how technical rules shape the order of public expression.
research_direction_en: It sits in the research area linking platform governance to democratic life, especially how technical rules shape the order of public expression.
raw_title: Platform Illiberalism on X: Governance, Visibility, and Political Signaling
raw_abstract: Using a case study of policy change, ranking signals, and public reaction on X, the paper argues that governance redesign can normalize asymmetrical visibility. The authors describe how moderation, recommendation, and symbolic leadership signals combine to reorganize who appears legitimate in platform publics.
